comments.text: Regarding the formula $f_2(3n^2+3n+1)=9n^2+6n$: Might there be a typo in [Er75f] and it should read $f_2(3n^2+3n+1)=9n^2+3n$? This would be https://oeis.org/A045945 and the right thing if we are looking for the number of edges in the $n$-hexagonal graph/triangular lattice. This came up when AlphaProof found a disproof of this for the case $n=1$. Could also be an unrelated misformalisation, but is there are construction where the number $9n^2 + 6n$ appears and not $9n^2 + 3n$? This is also mentioned in the next sentence in [Er75f]. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
$f_2(3n^2+3n+1)=9n^2+3n$ is consistent with the next equation $f_2(n)=\lfloor 3n-\sqrt{12n-3}\rfloor$.
[BeKa18] is unable to load any reference. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)

GPT 5.2 Pro has identified some tighter bounds in the literature: https://chatgpt.com/share/696fa4e5-0658-8007-af1d-308de8ab1432 It doesn't provide any commentary beyond directly pasting the result from each Arxiv paper. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
ChatGPT mostly confirms and cleans up results from that paper.
